column1: Sentence
CRYPTOLOCKER
Technical Specifications
PURPOSE
To develop a simple and efficient minimalistic cryptolocker.
REQUIREMENTS
- Minimum binary size
- Compliance with the requirements in "code and assembly design"
This includes making the program look like a dll, and having an entry point for Cobalt Strike
- Having a builder (configurator) that flashes the settings and creates a "locker-enlocker" pair
- Using a fast streaming cipher (ChaCha20 or similar), to maximize the speed and speed of system coverage.
The key management scheme can follow REvil/Sodinokibi https://blog.amossys.fr/sodinokibi-malware-analysis.html.
(symmetric key for covering Chacha20 files is generated at locker startup;
it is encrypted with the RSA4096 public key embedded in the locker and stored on the disk of the covered machine;
RSA4096 private key is sewn into the locker, it allows to read and decrypt the ChaCha20 symmetric key used for covering).
- the program must cover all available network balls
- the streaming model of the program should maximize disk and network balloons processing
- the key quality of the program is its speed of disk processing.
PROTECTIVE MEASURES
- Obfuscation of strings and system calls
- Removal of AV hooks at startup
- Mitigations for injection protection
- Injection protection by BaseThreadInitThunk interception
- protection against process stopping (check under WOW64!)
- protection against computer restart while program is running (check under WOW64!)
- removal of shadow copies at startup (check under WOW64!)
- disabling Windows recovery mode
IDENTIFICATION
Bot is identified by a pair of
1. dev-id, which is calculated as a hash function from unique system characteristics - hardware and OS.
Purpose: to identify the computer.
2. an encryption key flashed during the build phase.
Purpose: to identify the target under attack, to find an interlocker for a specific target using this fingerprint.
Details:
It is suggested to use md5/sha hash from the string "creation_date%windir%.computer_name.creation_date%windir\system32%.domain_name_or_workgroup".
This can also include hard disk name, MAC address of network interfaces, and other hardware names.
The key properties of dev-id are
- the dev-id must be generated every time
- it must be the same when running from different users on the system
- it must not be saved to disk
- it must be unique
- it must always be generated the same way on the same computer.
Bilder creates a pair of executable files "locker-anlocker", generating a pair of crypto keys for them and flashing them into the files.
Wherever the key is used for identification purposes (not for disk encryption purposes), a short fingerprint of the key must be used.
BILDER
Bilder is a console program that
- takes as input two files, a locker and an anlocker
- generates a pair of crypto-keys, exports them to the files.
- flashes these keys into the locker and the anlocker.
- it is necessary to be able to generate a pair of keys and use a pair of previously created keys (from files).
- and also flashes into them other settings specified from the command line (see item 9 of the ALGORITHM section).
- outputs flashed locker and unlocker with changed names.
The name of the locker and the anchor must include the key fingerprint and the date of creation, e.g., locker_aabbccdd:
locker_aabbccddeeff_01012020.ex_
- the file extension on the output should be .ex_ to prevent accidental startup!!!
ALGORITHM
1. Check the keyboard layout, and if it matches any country from the CIS + Ukraine zone, terminate immediately.
This feature should be disabled via conditional compilation.
2. Generate your dev-id
3. Generate a key fingerprint
4. Generate the text for the in-memory lander file by substituting your id's.
(omission left here intentionally)
The program works in one of two modes: fast or full.
In fast mode, only the first megabyte of the file is encrypted.
This is necessary to cover the system quickly.
In full mode, the entire file is encrypted.
There are settings for fast mode:
- percentage of file coverage
- maximum file size for full encryption (after this size the file is partially encrypted).
These settings are best set in the command line.
When partially covering a file, it should be done either in staggered order (the first 1M is covered, then 1M is skipped, 1M is covered again, etc.),
or according to some predictable formula (golden section progression, so that the maximum covered space is not concentrated
only in one place of the file)
8.3.1 After the catalog has been processed, a landing file with the text about redemption is created in it.
8.4 If an access error of Share violation type occurs (the file is occupied by another process),
the program finds the blocking process and kills it, or stops the corresponding service.
In case of errors, the program tries to repeat the action three times with an interval of 2 minutes, after which it skips the file.
Further work does not depend on the result of this step.
The program first processes directories from a special "fast" list - the list of directories,
which must be passed through first.
8.6 The program DOES NOT touch files and directories from a special stop list - a list of files that cannot be touched.
The combinations of quick list and stop list are handled as follows: (*)
8.6.1 We cover all paths from the quick list that are not in the stop list
8.6.2 If the entire disk is in the stop list, we cover only the paths from the quick list on that disk, without touching the rest of the disk
8.6.3 If the stop list is empty, cover the folders from the quick list first; cover the rest of the files afterwards.
* see also item 12 on network modes
The program encrypts only files with extensions from the list of working extensions; the rest are ignored.
8.8 The program deletes files from the special list by overwriting their contents three times:
- first time with constant 0
- second time with constant FF
- third time with random garbage
- the fourth time the file is deleted
The program processes all disks in this way.
All errors are ignored.
The term "all disks" also includes all available network Shares as well as network disks.
List of settings to be changed in the program:
Operating mode (fast/full)
encryption key
quick list
stop list
9.6. list of working extensions
delete list
lending text
All listings can be either directory paths or individual files.
All file-related settings must support:
- wildcards (* character)
- environment variables.
There should be provision for handling files without extension (by default, files without extension should be handled).
All lists should be validated for correctness (obviously incorrect paths should be ignored).
The program shall self-destruct upon termination.
There should be two counters in the test assembly:
how much data is encrypted, in bytes
the size of processed files, including those that are partially encrypted, skipped, deleted, etc. - this is an indicator of the overall processing speed.
These counters should be periodically output to the log, along with the current timestamp.
This is necessary for speed measurements.
Additionally, the following modes of scanning network resources/file balloons should be provided
local - Encrypts only local files + priority list
net - Encrypts only network resources + priority list.
The list of network resources is specified in a file, in ip/host address format, one line for each host.
all - Encrypts as net + local (set by default)
scan - Encrypts as net + automatic network scanning by subnet mask
scanext - Host list is set in the file + as scan
Parameter can only be specified with -m net or without the -m parameter.
The file with hosts must contain ip/host address on one line for each host.
Network scanning and balloon processing takes a long time, so processing of local files
in network modes should begin immediately.
Note that the same network disks may be mounted as local disks and be detected as a separate network balloon.
In such a case, disk re-encryption should be prevented.
In general, great care should be taken to detect re-encryption of an already covered directory/resource,
since encryption can and will be launched in parallel from multiple computers.
LENDING FILE
This is a text file with the ransomware text.
The name is readme.<6 random letters and numbers>.txt.
The macros used in the file are:
%devid% - dev-id of the computer
%fingerprint% - key fingerprint.
The values created in steps 2 and 3 are substituted instead.
The text of the file is set from the builder.
KNOWN ERRORS AND LIMITATIONS OF WINDOWS
- when trying to open a file with WOW64 process on 64-bit Windows 7/8/2009,
CreateFile/OpenFile functions always return TRUE status and do not return error code.
at the same time a crash is possible when trying to read/write.
As a solution to the problem, the number of open process descriptors before and after an attempt to open a file is checked.
- WOW64-process cannot get a valid list of all descriptors in the system on 64-bit Windows XP/2003
- when a file is opened by a WOW64 process with FILE_FLAG_OVERLAPPED flag,
a file can be opened almost simultaneously by several read/write processes.
At the same time, the file may be sequentially overwritten by all these processes.
Be careful when running multiple lockers at the same time!
Named mutex will only help with local disks!
- High buffer access latency when using virtual memory, especially noticeable on Windows 10.
Better to allocate memory from the heap.
- File system/file corruption can cause hangs when processing such files
- Some encryption algorithms slow down on files with high entropy (media - .mp3, .mp4, .avi, archives, etc.)
DATA SOURCES

More alive than ever: analyze the first sample of the new BlackMatter encryptor
https://habr.com/ru/company/group-ib/blog/571940/
TEST PLAN
1. Functionality
Testing is conducted with AV disabled.
All encryption modes and configuration variations are tried.
The following are confirmed:
software response to settings
compliance with the specified encryption mode
correct processing of network disks and orbs
correct processing of several simultaneously running instances of the software on one computer
the same as 1.4, but the same network balloon is processed from different computers
1.6. deletion of shadow copies of operating system volumes
preserving OS operability after shutdown
stopping the locker from a normal user
stopping the locker from an administrator
preventing machine reboot/stop
2. Compatibility
The test is done on the following Windows OS versions:
Windows 10
Windows Server 2012-2018
Windows 8.1
Windows 7
Windows Server 2008 R2
Windows Server 2008 (without R2)
Windows XP
Windows Server 2003
3. Speed
The speed of operation is measured.
For this purpose, the software should have built-in statistics metrics - you need a way to find out the speed
in megabytes per second
file descriptors per second (since files may be small).
Of course, we make a correction for the fact that the test is conducted on virtual machines, but we can compare the indicators with competitors' software on the same VM.
4. antiviruses
Windows Defender
ESET
Sophos
Avast
BitDefender
Norton
Kaspersky
Absence of detections 4.1-4.3 is mandatory.
The mode when AB makes a behavioral detection but cannot remove the process until the end of its processing is acceptable.
